title: ophrys × demutiana
intro: Ophrys × demutiana, also known as the bee orchid, is a hybrid plant species within the Ophrys genus, characterized by its unique and fragrant flowers that mimic the appearance and scent of female bees to attract pollinators. Traditionally, the bee orchid has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the bee orchid is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb have not been extensively scientifically studied.
